description: Business-logic vulnerability SUBCLASSES + their concrete test patterns and hot parameters, curated from a corpus of disclosed business-logic reports. Sharper than the flat class priors: it tells the logic modeler WHICH sub-flaw to test and WHERE. `cases` is corpus prevalence (count) — NOT a fabricated severity %, which the corpus does not cleanly provide; `severity` is the class/pattern-inherited grade. No payloads or PII.

patterns: reset token / code echoed in the response or a redirect
reset token not bound to the requesting user (swap victim id)
reset flow step skippable (jump straight to set-password)
sequential/UUID object-id traversal reads another user's data
resource-id replacement (swap id A -> id B across accounts)
admin-only endpoint reachable by a low-priv identity
captcha not refreshed / reusable across requests
captcha absent on the sensitive endpoint
login has no rate-limit / lockout (mass credential replay)
user-enumeration oracle in login/reset error messages
signature/timestamp check replayable or forgeable (sign, ts)
state/step flag trusts the client (status=paid, verified=1)
